---
title: "MVVM Architecture по экранам"
format: html
---

# MVVM Architecture по экранам

Detailed screen-by-screen architecture with MVVM pattern.

## Screen Architecture Pattern

Each screen follows this consistent MVVM pattern:

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-mvvm-pattern
%%| fig-cap: "MVVM паттерн для экранов"
graph TB
    User([User]) --> Screen[Screen Composable]
    
    Screen --> VM[ViewModel]
    VM --> UseCase[Use Cases]
    UseCase --> Repo[Repository]
    Repo --> Data[(Data Sources)]
    
    Data -.Flow.-> Repo
    Repo -.Flow.-> UseCase
    UseCase -.Flow.-> VM
    VM -.StateFlow.-> Screen
    Screen --> UI[UI Updates]
    
    style Screen fill:#e3f2fd
    style VM fill:#fff3e0
    style Repo fill:#ffecb3
    style Data fill:#c8e6c9
```

## UiState Pattern

All screens use sealed interface for state:

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-uistate
%%| fig-cap: "UiState паттерн с состояниями"
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> Success: Data loaded
    Loading --> Error: Load failed
    
    Success --> Loading: Refresh
    Error --> Loading: Retry
    
    state Success {
        [*] --> Empty
        Empty --> HasData: Data available
        HasData --> Empty: Data cleared
    }
```

## Screen Implementations

### 1. LibraryScreen

```kotlin
// State
sealed interface LibraryUiState {
    data object Loading : LibraryUiState
    data class Success(
        val books: List<Book>,
        val sortOrder: SortOrder
    ) : LibraryUiState
    data class Error(val message: String) : LibraryUiState
}

// ViewModel
@HiltViewModel
class LibraryViewModel @Inject constructor(
    private val getBooksUseCase: GetBooksUseCase,
    private val deleteBooksUseCase: DeleteBookUseCase
) : ViewModel() {
    val uiState: StateFlow<LibraryUiState> = ...
}

// Screen
@Composable
fun LibraryScreen(
    onBookClick: (String) -> Unit,
    viewModel: LibraryViewModel = hiltViewModel()
) {
    val uiState by viewModel.uiState.collectAsStateWithLifecycle()
    // UI rendering
}
```

### 2. PlayerScreen

```kotlin
// State
data class PlayerUiState(
    val book: Book? = null,
    val isPlaying: Boolean = false,
    val currentPosition: Long = 0,
    val duration: Long = 0,
    val playbackSpeed: Float = 1.0f,
    val chapters: List<Chapter> = emptyList()
)

// ViewModel
@HiltViewModel
class PlayerViewModel @Inject constructor(
    private val audioService: AudioPlayerService,
    private val getBooksUseCase: GetBookUseCase
) : ViewModel() {
    val uiState: StateFlow<PlayerUiState> = ...
}
```

### 3. SearchScreen

```kotlin
// State
sealed interface SearchUiState {
    data object Empty : SearchUiState
    data object Searching : SearchUiState
    data class Results(
        val localBooks: List<Book>,
        val onlineBooks: List<SearchResult>
    ) : SearchUiState
    data class Error(val message: String) : SearchUiState
}
```

### 4. DownloadsScreen

```kotlin
// State
data class DownloadsUiState(
    val activeDownloads: List<Download> = emptyList(),
    val completedDownloads: List<Download> = emptyList()
)

// Download state
data class Download(
    val id: String,
    val title: String,
    val status: DownloadStatus,
    val progress: Float,
    val speed: Long,
    val eta: Long
)

enum class DownloadStatus {
    QUEUED, DOWNLOADING, PAUSED, COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELLED
}
```

## ViewModel Lifecycle

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-viewmodel-lifecycle
%%| fig-cap: "Жизненный цикл ViewModel"
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Created
    Created --> Active: init block
    
    state Active {
        [*] --> Collecting
        Collecting --> Collecting: Data updates
        Collecting --> Updating: User action
        Updating --> Collecting: Update complete
    }
    
    Active --> Cleared: Activity destroyed
    Cleared --> [*]: onCleared()
```

## Data Flow Example

### User clicks book in Library

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-data-flow
%%| fig-cap: "Поток данных при клике на книгу"
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant UI as LibraryScreen
    participant VM as LibraryViewModel
    participant Nav as NavController
    participant PS as PlayerScreen
    participant PVM as PlayerViewModel
    participant Service as AudioPlayerService
    
    User->>UI: Click book
    UI->>Nav: navigate(PlayerRoute(bookId))
    Nav->>PS: Create screen
    PS->>PVM: Get ViewModel
    PVM->>Service: loadBook(bookId)
    Service-->>PVM: Book loaded
    PVM-->>PS: Update UI state
    PS-->>User: Show player
```

## Dependency Injection in Screens

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-di-screens
%%| fig-cap: "Dependency Injection для экранов"
graph TB
    Screen[Screen Composable] --> HiltVM[hiltViewModel]
    HiltVM --> VM[ViewModel @HiltViewModel]
    VM --> DI[Hilt DI]
    
    DI --> UC[Use Cases @Inject]
    DI --> Repo[Repositories @Inject]
    DI --> Service[Services @Inject]
    
    VM -.@Inject.-> UC
    VM -.@Inject.-> Repo
    VM -.@Inject.-> Service
    
    style Screen fill:#e3f2fd
    style VM fill:#fff3e0
    style DI fill:#f3e5f5
```

## Common Patterns

### Loading Pattern
```kotlin
viewModelScope.launch {
    _uiState.value = UiState.Loading
    try {
        val data = repository.getData()
        _uiState.value = UiState.Success(data)
    } catch (e: Exception) {
        _uiState.value = UiState.Error(e.message)
    }
}
```

### Flow Collection Pattern
```kotlin
repository.getBooksFlow()
    .map { books -> UiState.Success(books) }
    .catch { e -> emit(UiState.Error(e.message)) }
    .stateIn(
        scope = viewModelScope,
        started = SharingStarted.WhileSubscribed(5000),
        initialValue = UiState.Loading
    )
```

### Event Handling Pattern
```kotlin
fun onBookClick(bookId: String) {
    viewModelScope.launch {
        // Handle event
    }
}
```
